short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $0.35B in FY2025 Q4, +48.3% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $0.27B (-22.9% vs current Q) — set by 17 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $-0.10.
Next earnings: 2026-09-04 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $1B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $1B → FY2 $1B (+23.8% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: +65.0% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 148.7% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 0.12 — call-heavy positioning (consensus bullish).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 74.0% across 4 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 4 dates spanning 119–609 DTE.
238-day implied move: ±57.8% ($28.11 – $105.07) — the long-dated 1-σ range.

Term structure in backwardation (-74.7pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $60 put / short $73 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
